violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. Raw chicken stored above raw fish in containers in the walk-in cooler. Raw eggs being stored above ready to eat foods in the walk-in cooler.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Wet towels left out on the cutting boards on the cook line.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooked potentially hazardous food was not cooled properly from 70F to 41F or below within four hours. 4 containers of ground beef cooked the day before were temping at 55-60 degrees in the walk-in cooler.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Shelf in the dish pit area covered in aluminum foil was flaking off and was not a cleanable surface. Carboard on shelving used to store food in the walk-in cooler is not a cleanable surface.
Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Employee was observed just rinsing the dishes and not putting them through the dish machine, or not sanitizing them in the 3 compartment sink.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. Food containers and dish cleaning utensils in the hand sink in the back prep area.
